behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on real neural translation model quality, which is non-deterministic and semantic. A mock or deterministic fixture can validate wiring but can hide real quality failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation API on a multilingual evaluation set (idioms, tense, gender, negation). Use human-rated or strong reference-based semantic adequacy/fluency checks with acceptance thresholds; fail if coherence/meaning drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: On primary API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and still returns success to caller.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: real HTTP 503 handling, retry/error mapping, and failover path can differ from in-process mocks. A real HTTP boundary is needed to catch transport/serialization/status-code handling failures.
behaviors.test_description: Start service with primary translator pointed to a test HTTP endpoint that returns 503. Verify request returns 200-equivalent success with fallback translation payload (no propagated upstream error), and that failover path is recorded.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: SQL schema/query/collation/connection issues are missed by pure unit mocks. No external network/OS complexity beyond local DB, so Integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real SQLite test DB with known phrase rows, invoke fallback translator through service module boundary, and assert exact returned translation for existing phrases (including case/diacritic variants relevant to schema rules).

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Computation alone could be Unit, but behavior includes persistence. MOCK-MISS applies for serialization/schema/write failures; local module+DB wiring is minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Execute translation comparison with controlled primary/fallback strings, assert exact edit-distance value, then read persistence store and verify metric row/fields (request id, both outputs, computed distance) were written correctly.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously in background.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: requires real primary API call behavior plus async/background execution timing. In-process mocks can miss race conditions and completion/flush issues in background logging.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with real HTTP primary translator endpoint and real SQLite store. Assert response contains primary translation, then poll/await background completion and confirm quality comparison record appears without blocking request latency beyond budget.
